---
title: "Traps & the Evidence Habit"
subtitle: "The one-page cross-cutting review — highest value for exam prep"
---

<!-- ============================================================
OUTLINE / SCAFFOLD. Consolidates every recurring misconception plus
the "how to show evidence" method. Generic examples only.
============================================================ -->

## Master trap list

<!-- TODO: one row per recurring Week 1 trap, with the fix. -->

| # | Trap | Reality / fix |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | displayed ≠ stored | a bare expression shows a value but stores nothing |
| 2 | planned ≠ current | nothing changes `current` until a line assigns to it |
| 3 | cast mutates source | `float(t)` returns a new value; `t` is unchanged |
| 4 | `+` adds strings | `"21.0" + "0.6"` → `"21.00.6"`, not `21.6` |
| 5 | truthiness = meaning | `bool("False")` is `True`; use `== "reviewed"` |
| 6 | inclusive vs. exclusive | the equals bar decides if the exact edge passes |
| 7 | `and` vs. `or` | `and` = all-required; `or` = at-least-one |
| 8 | `=` vs. `==` | `=` stores; `==` checks |

## The non-mutating check rule (say it once)

::: {.check-band}
✅ **A check is `print(x)` or a bare `x`. Never an assignment.** If there's an `=` (not `==`), you changed state instead of checking it.
:::

## How to show evidence — one move per verb

<!-- TODO: fill the "how" column with a concrete move. -->

| Verb | What it proves | A concrete move |
|---|---|---|
| **predict** | you know the output | write the exact printed/displayed value |
| **trace** | you track state | table of each variable after each line |
| **test** | you probe edges | below / exact / above the boundary |
| **debug** | you can fix a claim | name the wrong claim **and** the correction |
| **explain / transfer** | you can decide | one plain sentence tying evidence to the decision |

## Reusable condition-ledger template

| Check | Source | Value | Connector | Effect |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| … | … | … | … | … |

## Closeout sentence frame

<!-- TODO: keep this frame for any readiness write-up. -->

> "The record **is / is not** ready because ___ ; before closing, we need ___ ."
